serviceNotes.engine oil: The N63 4.4L twin-turbo V8 runs hotter than inline-6 variants due to its hot-vee configuration (turbochargers mounted between the cylinder banks). BMW CBS maximum is 15,000 km / 12 months but many specialists recommend 10,000 km intervals.
serviceNotes.micro filter: BMW CBS scheduled service. The cabin micro filter traps pollen, dust and pollutants.
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Brake fluid is hygroscopic and absorbs moisture over time, reducing its boiling point and braking effectiveness. Replacement is time-based — every 2 years regardless of mileage.
serviceNotes.air cleaner element: BMW CBS scheduled service. The N63 V8 uses two air filters (one per bank). Both should be replaced together.
serviceNotes.spark plugs: The N63 V8 uses 8 spark plugs (one per cylinder, plus 8 ignition coils). BMW specifies replacement around 60,000 km. The hot-vee layout can accelerate spark plug wear.
serviceNotes.check front brake pads: The M550i is a heavy performance sedan. The front brakes bear the majority of braking load and wear faster than the I6 540i variants.
serviceNotes.check rear brake pads: Rear brake pads typically last longer than fronts but require periodic inspection.
serviceNotes.vehicle check: BMW CBS scheduled inspection covering braking systems, vehicle diagnostics, and technical components.
serviceNotes.ZF8 gearbox fluid: ZF recommends fluid replacement every 100,000 km despite BMW's 'lifetime fill' classification. The N63's higher torque output makes this especially important.
serviceNotes.xDrive transfer case fluid: The xDrive transfer case distributes torque between front and rear axles. The M550i is xDrive-only.
serviceNotes.differential fluids: Differential fluids lubricate the gears that transfer power to the wheels. The M550i's sport differential benefits from fresh fluid.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Engine coolant loses anti-corrosion properties over time. The N63's hot-vee layout puts greater thermal stress on the cooling system — flush interval is shorter than I6 variants.
